facts.title: Parasite made Oscar history
facts.fact: Bong Joon-ho's **Parasite** (기생충) won Best Picture at the 2020 Academy Awards, becoming the **first non-English-language film** ever to take the top prize. It also won Best Director, Best Original Screenplay, and Best International Feature.
facts.korean_en: Parasite won an Oscar.
facts.note: **기생충** literally means "parasite." The director's name, 봉준호, is written family-name-first as in Korean order.

facts.title: Park Chan-wook and the Korean thriller
facts.fact: Director **Park Chan-wook** (박찬욱) is known worldwide for stylish, intense thrillers, especially **Oldboy** (올드보이), which won the Grand Prix at the 2004 Cannes Film Festival. Korean cinema has earned a global reputation for its bold thrillers.
facts.korean_en: Park Chan-wook is a famous director.
facts.note: **감독** means "director" and is also the word for a sports team's coach or manager.

facts.title: The Busan International Film Festival
facts.fact: The **Busan International Film Festival** (부산국제영화제) is one of Asia's largest film festivals. Held each autumn, it showcases new Asian and international cinema and draws filmmakers and fans from around the world.
facts.korean_en: A film festival is held in Busan.
facts.note: **영화제** combines 영화 (film) with 제 (festival); the festival is often shortened to **BIFF**.

facts.title: A nation of moviegoers
facts.fact: South Korea has one of the **highest cinema-attendance rates per person** in the world. Large multiplex theaters (영화관) fill city centers, and going to the movies is a popular outing for friends, couples, and families.
facts.korean_en: I watch a movie at the cinema.
facts.note: **영화관** literally means "film hall"; you may also hear the loanword **극장** for theater.

facts.title: Train to Busan and the zombie boom
facts.fact: The 2016 hit **Train to Busan** (부산행) is a zombie film set on a speeding train and became a worldwide success. It helped show that Korean horror and action could win huge international audiences.
facts.korean_en: I like scary movies.
facts.note: **부산행** means "bound for Busan" — 행 attached to a place name marks a train or flight's destination.

facts.title: From a small industry to a global force
facts.fact: Korean cinema dates back to the silent era of the early **1900s**, but it grew dramatically after the 1990s. Today Korean films and the directors behind them are celebrated at major festivals around the world.
facts.korean_en: Korean films are very interesting.

vocabulary.en: to be fun, to be interesting

facts.note: **한국 영화** means "Korean film"; the boom since the late 1990s is sometimes called the Korean cinema renaissance.

facts.title: Chungmuro, the heart of Korean film
facts.fact: The street name **Chungmuro** (충무로) in central Seoul became the historic hub of the Korean movie industry. Even today, "Chungmuro" is used as a nickname for Korean cinema, much like "Hollywood" stands for the American film world.
facts.korean_en: Chungmuro is the street of film.
facts.note: Many old film studios and offices once gathered near **충무로**, which is also a stop on the Seoul subway.

facts.title: The ten-million-admission club
facts.fact: In Korea, a movie that sells more than **ten million tickets** is called a **천만 영화** (ten-million film), a major mark of success in a country of about 50 million people. Hits like *The Host* and *Extreme Job* reached this milestone.
facts.korean_en: Ten million people watched this movie.
facts.note: **천만** is 천 (thousand) times 만 (ten thousand); reaching this number is a headline event for any Korean film.

facts.title: Lee Chang-dong, the novelist-director
facts.fact: **Lee Chang-dong** (이창동) is a former novelist whose quiet, deeply human films like *Poetry* and *Burning* are praised at festivals worldwide. His work is known for its literary feeling and emotional depth.
facts.korean_en: Director Lee Chang-dong also wrote novels.
facts.note: **소설** means "novel"; Lee Chang-dong even served for a time as Korea's Minister of Culture.

facts.title: Im Kwon-taek, a master of tradition
facts.fact: **Im Kwon-taek** (임권택) directed more than a hundred films over his long career and won Best Director at Cannes for **Chihwaseon**. His movies often explore Korean tradition, such as *Seopyeonje*, a story about pansori folk singing.
facts.korean_en: Director Im Kwon-taek made many movies.
facts.note: **전통** means "tradition"; his film *서편제* (Seopyeonje) helped renew public interest in pansori.

facts.title: Hong Sang-soo's small, talkative films
facts.fact: **Hong Sang-soo** (홍상수) makes many low-budget films built around long conversations, often featuring artists, drinking, and awkward romance. A regular at major festivals, he releases new work almost every year.
facts.korean_en: There is a lot of conversation in Hong Sang-soo's films.

vocabulary.en: to be many, to be a lot

facts.note: **대화** means "conversation"; his films are famous for natural, everyday dialogue rather than big action.

facts.title: Minari, a Korean-American story
facts.fact: **Minari** (미나리) is a 2020 American film about a Korean immigrant family farming in rural Arkansas. Veteran actress **Youn Yuh-jung** (윤여정) won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress for her role as the grandmother.
facts.korean_en: Minari is the story of a Korean family.
facts.note: **미나리** is the name of a Korean herb (water dropwort); Youn Yuh-jung was the first Korean to win an acting Oscar.

facts.title: The screen-quota system
facts.fact: Korea's **screen-quota system** (스크린쿼터) requires theaters to show domestic films for a set number of days each year. The policy was created to protect Korean cinema from being crowded out by foreign blockbusters.
facts.korean_en: Korean theaters also show Korean films.
facts.note: **스크린쿼터** is a loanword from English ("screen quota"); the required number of days has changed over the years.

facts.title: 4DX and ScreenX from Korea
facts.fact: The immersive cinema formats **4DX** and **ScreenX** were developed by a Korean company and are now used in theaters worldwide. 4DX adds moving seats, wind, and scents, while ScreenX wraps the picture across three walls.
facts.korean_en: In this cinema the seats move.
facts.note: **기술** means "technology"; these formats show how Korea innovates in the cinema experience, not just in films.

facts.title: The Blue Dragon Film Awards
facts.fact: The **Blue Dragon Film Awards** (청룡영화상) are among Korea's most prestigious movie prizes, held every year since 1963. Winning Best Film here is a major honor for Korean directors and actors.
facts.korean_en: The Blue Dragon Awards are a famous film award.
facts.note: **청룡** means "blue dragon"; the ceremony is broadcast on television and widely followed by fans.

facts.title: The Grand Bell Awards
facts.fact: The **Grand Bell Awards** (대종상) are Korea's oldest film awards, first held in 1962. Often compared to the American Oscars, they have honored generations of Korean filmmakers.
facts.korean_en: The Grand Bell Awards are an old film award in Korea.
facts.note: **대종상** literally refers to a "great bell"; it is one of the country's three big film-award ceremonies.

facts.title: Korean animation and Pororo
facts.fact: Korea has a strong animation industry, and the children's character **Pororo the Little Penguin** (뽀로로) is so beloved that he is jokingly called "the children's president." Korean studios also produce animation for shows around the world.
facts.korean_en: Children like Pororo.
facts.note: **만화 영화** literally means "comic film" and is a common Korean word for animation; the loanword **애니메이션** is also used.

facts.title: Independent and documentary film
facts.fact: Alongside big blockbusters, Korea has a lively **independent film** (독립영화) scene. The low-budget documentary *My Love, Don't Cross That River* became a surprise hit, drawing huge audiences to a quiet true story about an elderly couple.
facts.korean_en: I watched an independent film.
facts.note: **독립영화** combines 독립 (independence) with 영화 (film); small art-house theaters often screen these movies.

facts.title: Korean films at Cannes
facts.fact: Korean cinema has a remarkable record at the **Cannes Film Festival** (칸 영화제). In 2022 alone, Park Chan-wook won Best Director and Song Kang-ho won Best Actor, showing the strength of Korean filmmaking on the world stage.
facts.korean: 한국 영화가 칸 영화제에서 상을 받았어요.
facts.korean_en: A Korean film won an award at the Cannes Film Festival.
facts.note: **칸 영화제** is the Korean name for the Cannes Film Festival; 칸 is the transliteration of "Cannes."

facts.title: Song Kang-ho, a beloved leading man
facts.fact: **Song Kang-ho** (송강호) is one of Korea's most respected actors, starring in hits such as *Parasite*, *The Host*, and *Memories of Murder*. In 2022 he won Best Actor at Cannes for the film *Broker*.
facts.korean_en: Song Kang-ho is a good actor.
facts.note: **연기** means "acting"; Song Kang-ho has worked with many of Korea's top directors over his career.
